title: The Config Hunt (Capstone)
scenario: A new server has been handed to you. No documentation. You need to locate all config files, read critical values out of them using grep, and compile a report — combining find, grep, head, and tail together.
symptoms: Logs with errors and deploy events in /opt/myapp/
mission: Find all .conf files, extract DB_HOST, count ERROR lines in app.log, and capture the last 10 lines of deploy.log.

hints.title: Finding Configs
hints.content: Finding configs: Use `find /etc/myapp -name '*.conf'` to list all config files.

hints.title: Extracting Values
hints.content: Extracting values: Use `grep 'DB_HOST' /etc/myapp/database.conf` to pull out the database host line.

hints.title: Counting Errors
hints.content: Counting errors: Use `grep -c 'ERROR' /opt/myapp/app.log` to count error lines.

acceptance_criteria.description: /home/intern/error_count.txt exists and contains a number greater than 0
